ideas.title: What 'AI for your design studio' actually means
ideas.angle: Plain-English Take. Cut hype; explain in owner language where AI fits in an interior/architecture practice (mood boards, specs, BOQ, client updates). Sets up the Bridge 1 week.
ideas.notes: Keep it concrete with one interior example. No jargon.

ideas.title: I'm building an AI system for interior design firms
ideas.angle: Build-in-Public. Introduce the architectural automation. Name the ICP pain in their words (chasing vendor quotes, copy-pasting between five tools, hand-built mood boards/specs).
ideas.notes: First public Bridge 1 proof signal. Bridge metaphor hook.
ideas.retired_reason: Duplicate — post-006 (Setu means bridge) already did Bridge 1 intro, ICP pain, honest build status. Retired 2026-06-11.

ideas.title: The pipeline running: brief to plan-faithful render
ideas.angle: Build Receipt. Show mood board -> palette -> material/lighting/BOQ sheet -> plan-faithful render. Real output, organized + documented vs hand-built clutter.

asset.note: user supplies demo project + shareable render/sheets/screen-rec

ideas.notes: Anonymize any real client name. Wed proof asset format TBD (screen-rec vs carousel).

ideas.title: The AI kept moving the furniture
ideas.angle: Build-in-Public honesty post. V1->V4 render plan-faithfulness story: renders ignored the 2D plan; fixed with whitebox-PNG reference + strict layout language. 'The bad versions are the curriculum.'

asset.note: user supplies V1 vs V4 render images

ideas.notes: Strongest trust post of the week. Visual.
ideas.retired_reason: Duplicate — post-008 (Everyone keeps asking to see the renders) already covered V1 spatial failures story (sofa flipped, pooja vanished, invented doors). Retired 2026-06-11.

ideas.title: Stock-analysis recap + YouTube drop
ideas.angle: Build Receipt + promote YT1. Tie off the Week-1 stock-analysis thread; point to the full YouTube walkthrough.
ideas.notes: First YouTube upload. Promote once on LinkedIn.
ideas.retired_reason: Redundant — n8n story covered by post-004 (native Loom). Post-005-008 pivot to Bridge 01 ICP makes stock-analysis YouTube promo a positioning leak. Retired 2026-06-08.

ideas.title: Get the basics right before chasing AI (wake-up call)
ideas.angle: Wake-up call for design/construction firms: organized files, a real LinkedIn profile, documented projects come before automation. Promotes YT2.
ideas.notes: LECTURE RISK — frame as 'here's what I keep seeing work', not 'you're doing it wrong'. Hard five-value check on Respect-the-owner before publish.

ideas.title: A free AI advisor for design & architecture firms
ideas.angle: Lead magnet. Share the free Gemini Gem (architecture/interior instructions) — owners can ask how to improve their business step by step. Give value, not a sales ask.

asset.note: Gem URL live — used in post-009. Unblocked 2026-06-11.

ideas.cta: free tool (the real CTA of the week)

ideas.title: The spatial method that made the AI render hold the floor plan
ideas.angle: Build Receipt that fulfills post-008's open promise. Stop asking the model to *style* the plan; hand it coordinates, then verify the output against a plan-coords contract. Show V1 (sofa flipped, dining sealed, invented doors) vs V2 (geometry holds) and the exact check that caught the lie.
ideas.proof_plan: Screen-record V1 vs V2 renders side by side + the plan-coords.json check that flags the mismatch.

supporting_research.headline: AI renders do not preserve exact proportions; dimensional accuracy not guaranteed
post-008 teased 'the exact spatial method that made the plan stick'

strategic_opinion.reason: Fulfills post-008's open promise; proof (V2 render + plan-coords.json) is ready; pure map-before-build/ship-don't-slide receipt aimed dead-centre at the ICP.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: ASSET READY (V1 vs V4 demo recorded 2026-06). Top priority — fulfills post-008 promise.

ideas.title: The gap isn't curiosity — 64% try AI, only 20% run it
ideas.angle: Plain-English Take on 2026 survey data (6% regular use, 8% practice-level despite 85% exposure; 33% blame poor software fit). Reframes the 'you're behind' panic: the gap isn't the owner, it's tools built for the demo not the real workflow. Setu's counter = build to the workflow.
ideas.proof_plan: Cite the named 2026 surveys + contrast with one screenshot of the workflow-fit system running on a real 3BHK.

supporting_research.headline: Only 6% of architects regularly use AI; 8% of firms at practice level despite 85% exposure
33% cite poor compatibility with current software as the key obstacle

strategic_opinion.reason: On-positioning (extends post-006 'not behind, buried'); rests on the named 2026 Chaos x Architizer ~800-architect survey (auditable). Original '6% use AI' hook was WRONG and dropped 2026-06-13.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Stats verified 2026-06-13: use 64/20 gap + 33/48/70/74; do NOT use 6% or 85%-exposure.

ideas.title: An AI render in under a minute is easy. A render you can build from is the hard part.
ideas.angle: Contrarian / honesty post. The market sells one-minute photoreal renders; the dimensions are a polite lie (rooms stretched, doors invented). The trap: a client approves something that can't be built and trust dies on site day. Sequenced AFTER the spatial-method post so it stands on shown proof.
ideas.proof_plan: Reuse the V1 spatial-failure frames as the 'beautiful but wrong' exhibit; contrast with the verified V2.

supporting_research.headline: AI tools stretch or compress room dimensions interpreting floor plans
AI render in under a minute marketed as turnkey

strategic_opinion.reason: Strong and timely, but overlaps post-008 and is the FIRST Contrarian-pillar post — it must land after the method post (idea-008) so it reads as earned proof, not a repeat complaint.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Unlocks once idea-008 is published; Contrarian pillar gate (2-3 Build Receipts) already met.

ideas.title: I built a free AI advisor for design and architecture firms. Ask it anything.
ideas.angle: Build-in-Public lead magnet, value-first. The live Gemini Gem coaches a firm owner step by step on where AI fits their practice — no sales ask. Standalone dedicated post (post-009 only surfaced it in an auto first-comment).
ideas.proof_plan: Share the live Gem link + a 20-sec screen-rec of one real owner question answered well.

supporting_research.headline: Gem live at https://da.gd/z52I, already used in post-009 first comment

strategic_opinion.reason: Gem is now live (was the week's blocker); pure give-value, no ask, on-ICP. Mild repeat risk vs the post-009 comment — frame as a standalone utility with a concrete example.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Value-first; one concrete example question, not a feature list.

ideas.title: My whole content engine runs on about a cent a day
ideas.angle: Build-in-Public cost receipt. While Claude Fable 5 lands at $10/$50 per M tokens, the router sends ~90% of work to free/near-free models (gemma $0.00, gpt-4o-mini $0.0001) and saves the expensive model for judgment only. Real model_spend.json numbers.
ideas.proof_plan: Screenshot model_spend.json: gpt-4o $0.0069, gpt-4o-mini $0.0001, gemma $0.00 — daily total.

supporting_research.headline: Claude Fable 5 GA Jun 9 at $10/$50 per M tokens

strategic_opinion.reason: Positioning leak — it's builder-flex, off the design/construction ICP and drifts toward generic AI-builder content. Real and good, but it doesn't help an architecture owner cross the bridge.
strategic_opinion.when_to_post: after first client (reframe as 'how we keep your automation cheap') or repackage for a builder audience
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Reframe to client value before posting; as-is it's off-ICP.

ideas.title: Map the workflow before you buy the AI tool
ideas.angle: Plain-English Take on the 'map before build' value, anchored to the 33%-blame-software-fit stat. Owners buy a tool then bend the firm around it; the fix is a one-page map of who does what and where the hours leak, before any tool. Shows the thinking, not a product.
ideas.proof_plan: Show a real anonymized one-page workflow map of a firm's brief→delivery with the leak points circled.

supporting_research.headline: 33% cite poor software compatibility as the key AI obstacle

strategic_opinion.reason: On-value (map before build), respects owners, but lower new-info — good calendar filler, not a tentpole.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Needs a clean anonymized workflow-map graphic.

ideas.title: The four foundations, by the numbers
ideas.angle: Extends post-009 (Stop Working for Free). The four foundations — documented work, organized files, visible proof, one source of truth — aren't soft advice; the cost of missing them is measured. ~Half of local contractors have no website (no visible proof). Construction loses ~$31B/yr to poor comms + bad data; teams burn 14 hrs/week on rework and 5.5 hrs/week hunting for the right file. Fix the foundations before automating.
ideas.proof_plan: Cite named sources (FMI/PlanGrid rework data; 2026 SMB website survey) + tie to post-009's four foundations. Optional simple stat card.

supporting_research.headline: 45-56% of local contractors/tradespeople have no website
Miscommunication + bad data = 48% of rework; ~$31.3B/yr construction cost; 14h/wk rework, 5.5h/wk hunting data
~27% of small businesses have no website; 62% of customers ignore no-web-presence businesses

strategic_opinion.reason: Extends the published post-009 four-foundations theme, dead-on ICP, and fully backed by auditable public stats. Risk is stat-dump — cap at 3 figures + the human point.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Use ONLY verified figures; label construction-industry stats as such, do not imply interior-design-specific. No fabricated composite 'four-pillars readiness' stat — it does not exist publicly.
